hero.headline: JIIT Online MBA in Human Resource Management: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: NAAC A | UGC-DEB Approved | Deemed University est. 2001 | Total fees ₹1,75,000

sections.tldr: JIIT Online MBA in Human Resource Management covers managing employee relations and employment laws, workplace diversity/equity/inclusion, performance and compensation management, learning and development, organisational development and change, leadership skills for managers, competency management, and talent management. Mandatory Internship/OJT in Sem 3. AI in Business core. Total all-in ₹1,82,000. Zero-cost EMI available. No scholarships. No bundled perks.

about.body:
JIIT's Online MBA in Human Resource Management covers both operational HR (employee relations, employment laws, compensation, L&D) and strategic HR (OD and change, leadership, competency management, talent management). The inclusion of Workplace Diversity, Equity and Inclusion (DEI) as a standalone subject is notable. Most competing HR MBAs cover DEI as a module within OB or HRM, not as a dedicated course.

Semester 3 has 9 subjects including mandatory Internship/OJT and AI in Business. The HR-specific subjects cover employment laws (Industrial Disputes Act, new labour codes), DEI frameworks, performance management systems, compensation design, L&D programme design, OD interventions, and leadership development. Semester 4 adds competency management with assessment centre design and talent management strategy.

JIIT Noida is a deemed university established in 2001 with a tech-adjacent identity. NAAC A accredited, UGC-DEB approved. At ₹1,82,000 all-in, it is mid-tier with no scholarships and no bundled perks.

EdifyEdu verifies data independently and takes no commission from any university.

syllabus.note: Semesters 1-2 cover the common MBA core. Semesters 3-4 below show the Human Resource Management specialisation subjects.

subjects.name: Artificial Intelligence in Business
subjects.desc: AI applications in HR automation, ML for talent analytics, chatbots in employee services, and ethical AI in people management.

subjects.name: Internship/OJT
subjects.desc: Mandatory industry internship or on-the-job training with faculty supervision and evaluation.

subjects.name: Corporate Skill Development-2
subjects.desc: Advanced professional skills: executive presentation, conflict resolution, cross-functional team facilitation, and business writing.

subjects.name: Managing Employee Relations and Employment Laws
subjects.desc: Industrial Disputes Act, Factories Act, new labour codes (Code on Wages, IR Code), grievance handling, disciplinary proceedings, and statutory compliance.

subjects.name: Workplace Diversity, Equity and Inclusion
subjects.desc: DEI frameworks, unconscious bias awareness, inclusive hiring practices, accessibility compliance, gender equity policy design, and DEI metrics.

subjects.name: Performance and Compensation Management
subjects.desc: Performance appraisal systems (360-degree, OKR, balanced scorecard), compensation structure design, variable pay, ESOPs, and statutory compliance (PF, ESI, gratuity).

subjects.name: Learning and Development
subjects.desc: Training needs analysis, learning programme design, e-learning and blended learning, Kirkpatrick ROI evaluation, and leadership development programmes.

subjects.name: Organizational Development and Change
subjects.desc: OD intervention types, planned change models (Kotter, ADKAR), resistance management, culture transformation, and measuring OD effectiveness.

subjects.name: Leadership Skills for Managers
subjects.desc: Leadership styles and situational leadership, emotional intelligence in management, team building, coaching and mentoring, and executive presence.

subjects.name: Strategic Management
subjects.desc: Corporate strategy, competitive advantage analysis, strategic planning, and strategic control mechanisms.

subjects.name: Major Project
subjects.desc: Comprehensive HR project integrating Sem 1-4 learning. Industry-linked problem with faculty evaluation.

subjects.name: Competency Management and Assessment Centre
subjects.desc: Competency framework design, competency mapping, assessment centre methodology, behavioural event interviewing, and talent calibration.

subjects.name: Talent Management
subjects.desc: Talent acquisition strategy, succession planning, high-potential identification, employee value proposition design, and retention analytics.

whoHires.body:
JIIT MBA Human Resource Management graduates target HR roles across industries:

**Industries:** IT services, manufacturing, banking, FMCG, retail, healthcare, consulting (HR advisory), and government enterprises

**Entry-level roles:** HR Executive, Recruitment Executive, Compensation Analyst, L&D Coordinator, Employee Relations Executive, DEI Coordinator

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years experience):** HR Manager, Talent Acquisition Manager, Compensation Manager, HR Business Partner, L&D Manager, OD Manager

**Senior roles (7+ years):** HR Director, CHRO, VP HR, Head of Talent Management, Head of DEI, Head of OD

**Employers:** IT (TCS, Infosys, Wipro, HCL HR teams), manufacturing (Tata Motors, Maruti, L&T), banking (HDFC, ICICI HR), FMCG (HUL, Nestle, ITC HR), and NCR-region corporates.

**Note:** JIIT provides career support. Online MBA brand recognition is developing.

skills.body:
The HR specialisation builds skills across people management, compliance, and talent strategy:

**Technical skills:**
- Employment Law: Industrial Disputes Act interpretation, new labour code implementation, statutory compliance filing, and disciplinary proceeding documentation
- Compensation Design: job evaluation, pay band construction, variable pay structure, ESOP administration, and PF/ESI/gratuity compliance
- Assessment Centre: competency framework construction, BEI (Behavioural Event Interview) design, assessment centre exercise design, and calibration methodology
- DEI: inclusive hiring audit, unconscious bias training design, DEI dashboard metrics, and accessibility compliance assessment

**Applied skills:**
- L&D: training needs analysis, learning path design, e-learning evaluation, and ROI calculation for training spend
- Talent Management: succession planning grid, high-potential identification criteria, EVP design, and attrition root cause analysis

**Soft skills:** Handling sensitive employee relations cases, facilitating OD interventions, presenting HR strategy to boards, coaching managers on performance conversations, and leading DEI culture change.

comparisons.body:
Comparing JIIT Online MBA HR against peer programmes:

**vs Amity Online MBA HRM:** Amity at approximately ₹2,25,000 with NIRF #22. JIIT at ₹1,82,000 saves ₹43,000. Amity has larger alumni and broader spec variety. JIIT has DEI as a standalone subject (rare), mandatory internship, and leadership skills as a dedicated course. Amity wins on brand. JIIT wins on HR curriculum breadth and fee.

**vs MUJ Online MBA (no dedicated HR spec listed):** MUJ at approximately ₹1,80,000 does not list HR as a standalone spec in its current catalogue. JIIT's dedicated HR spec with 9 Sem 3 subjects is more directly relevant for HR career targeting.

**vs DPU Pune Online MBA HRM:** DPU at ₹1,89,400 with NIRF #41 and NAAC A++. JIIT at ₹1,82,000 saves ₹7,400. DPU has 7 Sem 3 HR subjects and stronger NAAC grade (A++ vs A). JIIT has DEI and Leadership as standalone subjects plus mandatory internship. DPU wins on accreditation. JIIT competes on curriculum.

**vs IGNOU Online MBA HRM:** IGNOU at ₹66,000 with NAAC A++. IGNOU has strong industrial relations depth for manufacturing and government. JIIT at ₹1,82,000 offers DEI, AI in Business, mandatory internship, and contemporary HR content (assessment centres, competency management) that IGNOU's MMPH curriculum does not cover.

fees.body:
The JIIT Online MBA in Human Resource Management has a total all-in cost of ₹1,82,000.

**Semester-wise fee breakdown:**
- Semester 1: ₹44,500 (₹42,000 course + ₹1,500 exam + ₹1,000 registration)
- Semester 2: ₹43,500 (₹42,000 course + ₹1,500 exam)
- Semester 3: ₹43,500 (₹42,000 course + ₹1,500 exam)
- Semester 4: ₹43,500 (₹42,000 course + ₹1,500 exam)
- **Grand total: ₹1,75,000 + ₹6,000 + ₹1,000 = ₹1,82,000**

**Zero-cost EMI options:**
- Semester: ₹7,417/month (6 EMIs)
- Annual: ₹8,800/month (10 EMIs)
- Full Course: ₹9,723/month (18 EMIs)

**Scholarships:** None offered.
**Bundled perks:** None.

Fees listed are indicative. EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with Jaypee JIIT each quarter. Please reconfirm with our counsellor before any payment.

certificate.body:
Graduates receive an MBA degree certificate with Human Resource Management as the specialisation, issued by Jaypee Institute of Information Technology (JIIT), Noida under UGC-DEB 2020 online mode standards.

The degree carries NAAC A accreditation and UGC-DEB approval. Valid for government HR officer recruitment, PSU applications, and private-sector HR roles. WES recognised. DigiLocker issuance is standard.

reviews.intro: Three reviews from JIIT Online MBA HR students. Ratings reflect individual experience.

items.name: Meera T.
items.liked: DEI as a standalone subject is genuinely rare at this price point. Most online MBAs treat diversity as a module within OB, not a full course
items.disliked: Online MBA brand recognition is the main concern. Recruiters know JIIT for BTech, not for management programmes
items.body: I work in HR at an IT company in Noida. The employee relations, compensation, and DEI subjects were directly applicable. The mandatory internship worked well since I aligned it with a project at my company. At ₹1,82,000 the value is solid for the curriculum depth. The limitation is brand positioning. For NCR-based HR roles, the JIIT name is known but not specifically for MBA.

items.name: Rajesh M.
items.liked: Leadership Skills for Managers was practically useful. Assessment Centre methodology in Sem 4 is directly applicable to my talent acquisition work
items.disliked: No bundled perks at all. Amity at ₹2,25,000 includes certifications. MUJ at ₹1,80,000 includes Coursera Plus. JIIT at ₹1,82,000 includes nothing extra
items.body: The HR curriculum is comprehensive. 9 Sem 3 subjects covering employment laws, DEI, compensation, L&D, OD, and leadership is more depth than most competitors offer. The AI in Business course helped me understand how AI-driven HR tools work. The gap is perks and brand. For the same fee as MUJ, I got more HR subjects but missed the Coursera Plus access.

items.name: Aarti S.
items.liked: Employment Laws module covered the new labour codes alongside existing acts, which is directly relevant to my compliance role
items.disliked: Career services for online learners are not as structured as on-campus. Job placement support needs improvement for the online programme
items.body: The HR curriculum is the strength. The OD and change management, talent management, and competency management subjects gave me frameworks I use in my HR consulting work. The mandatory internship was logistically tricky but manageable. My main concern is that at this fee point, JIIT should offer some form of career support or bundled certification to compete with MUJ and Amity.

faqs.question: Does the HR MBA cover DEI as a standalone subject?
faqs.answer: Yes. Workplace Diversity, Equity and Inclusion is a dedicated Semester 3 course covering DEI frameworks, unconscious bias, inclusive hiring, accessibility compliance, and DEI metrics. This is uncommon in online MBA programmes at this fee point.

faqs.question: Is the Internship/OJT mandatory?
faqs.answer: Yes. Internship/OJT is a credited Semester 3 component. Working professionals typically align it with an HR project at their current employer using the OJT option.

faqs.question: How does JIIT HR compare to DPU Pune HR?
faqs.answer: DPU at ₹1,89,400 has 7 Sem 3 subjects and NAAC A++. JIIT at ₹1,82,000 has 9 Sem 3 subjects including DEI and Leadership as standalones. DPU wins on accreditation. JIIT competes on curriculum breadth and fee.

faqs.question: Are there scholarships or perks?
faqs.answer: No scholarships and no bundled perks. The fee is ₹1,82,000 all-in with zero-cost EMI available. This is a gap compared to MUJ (Coursera Plus) and MAHE (Coursera Plus + 8 scholarship categories).

faqs.question: What is the total fee?
faqs.answer: ₹1,82,000 all-in (₹1,75,000 course + ₹6,000 exam + ₹1,000 registration). Zero-cost EMI from ₹7,417/month. Reconfirm with our counsellor before payment.

chooseIf: DEI, employment law, and assessment centre methodology as standalone subjects matter for your HR career.
Mandatory internship adds experiential value to your HR MBA.
₹1,82,000 all-in with zero-cost EMI fits your budget for comprehensive HR curriculum.
AI in Business core subject is relevant for HR tech and people analytics roles.
consider: Brand recognition matters for HR leadership roles. Amity, MAHE, and NMIMS have stronger MBA brand positioning.
MUJ at ₹1,80,000 offers Coursera Plus and Manipal brand at a similar fee.
You need scholarships. JIIT offers none.
DPU Pune at ₹1,89,400 with NAAC A++ has stronger accreditation for a similar fee.
